completion_summary: Corrected the approved-plan treatment for PL-036-258 and PL-038-264, and replaced the PL-037-261 source-evidence gap with the usable local source record while keeping the remaining laws at their source-supported note, transfer, repeal, or exclusion posture.

laws.title: Public Law 35-256 | Workers First Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The act remains operative, but the accessible text does not name a precise U.S. Code rewrite target for the tax-rate change.

provisions.summary: Short title and definitions.
provisions.basis: The bill may be cited as the Workers First Act of 2025 and defines the core terms.

provisions.summary: Reduces the income tax rate on American dollar income from 28 percent to 23 percent.
provisions.basis: The source says the tax rate shall be reduced from 28 percent to 23 percent, but no exact positive-law section rewrite is identified.

provisions.summary: Purpose and limitations clauses.
provisions.basis: The text states the policy goal and limitations but does not supply a codifiable rewrite target.

provisions.summary: Immediate enactment clause.
provisions.basis: The bill takes effect immediately after passage.

current_implementation.detail: The branch keeps the measure as a note-level entry at a plausible statutory anchor, but the dump/Trello link remain and no exact Code target is identified.

laws.recommended_action: Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link, and keep this as a statutory note unless a later source identifies a precise Code target.

laws.title: Public Law 36-256 | Commerce Dispersal Act
laws.status_basis: The act is an operative structural reorganization that transfers Commerce and Labor functions rather than expiring or being repealed.

provisions.summary: Short title and severability.
provisions.basis: The act may be cited as the Commerce Dispersal Act and includes severability.

provisions.summary: Dissolves Commerce and Labor and transfers its functions, enforcement, and related offices.
provisions.target: Department of Commerce and Labor and related successor offices
provisions.basis: The source says the Department of Commerce and Labor shall be rendered inoperable and its functions transferred.

provisions.summary: Transfers the Copyright Office and Copyright Royalty Board functions to the Department of State and the D.C. district court.
provisions.target: Library of Congress / Copyright Office / Copyright Royalty Board
provisions.basis: The source says the Copyright Office shall be transferred from the Library of Congress to the Department of State.

current_implementation.detail: The branch has a subject-matter transfer note, but it still needs to be reduced to concise transfer history and cleaned of dump/Trello baggage.

laws.recommended_action: Keep only a concise transfer note, remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link, and do not convert the restructuring into permanent positive-law sections.

laws.title: Public Law 36-257 | Maritime Operations & Policy Overhaul Act
laws.status_basis: The act is an ongoing corrective and transfer measure for maritime and Coast Guard policy, not a repealed enactment.

provisions.summary: Short title, purpose, and severability.
provisions.basis: The act may be cited as the Maritime Operations & Policy Overhaul Act and includes severability.

provisions.summary: Amends the Streamlining and Protecting American Security Act section 6(m).
provisions.target: Streamlining and Protecting American Security Act, section 6(m)
provisions.basis: Section 6(m) shall be amended to list the Coast Guard and subordinate units.

provisions.summary: Amends the Military Officer Commissioning Reform Act section 9(g).
provisions.target: Military Officer Commissioning Reform Act, section 9(g)
provisions.basis: Section 9(g) shall be amended to add the Coast Guard Commandant paragraph.

provisions.summary: Amends the Military Justice Reform Act of 2024 to redefine Armed Services.
provisions.target: Military Justice Reform Act of 2024, section 4(a)
provisions.basis: Section 4(a) of the Military Justice Reform Act of 2024 shall be amended to redefine Armed Services.

provisions.summary: Contingent Coast Guard assumption of duties and maritime-security coordination.
provisions.basis: The Coast Guard may assume duties and the Commandant serves as principal maritime security adviser.

current_implementation.detail: The branch parks the measure in a maritime note, but it still needs cleanup and should remain a concise amendment/transfer history.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the Coast Guard amendments as concise amendment notes, remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link, and do not fabricate a positive-law Code rewrite from the uncodified operational directives.

laws.title: Public Law 36-258 | National Root Beer Day Act
laws.status_basis: The holiday insertion is already effective and remains part of the federal-holiday list under 5 U.S.C. 6103(a).

provisions.summary: Short title and enactment clause.
provisions.basis: The bill may be cited as the National Root Beer Day Act and takes effect immediately.

provisions.summary: Amends 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) by inserting National Root Beer Day after Memorial Day.
provisions.basis: The source says 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) is amended by inserting after Memorial Day, the last Monday in May, the new holiday.

current_implementation.detail: The approved plan confirms the exact 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) insertion and parent-section note, and the branch matches origin/main.

laws.recommended_action: Retain the 5 U.S.C. 6103(a) insertion, remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link, and keep the short-title and effective-date language only as a note.

laws.title: Public Law 36-260 | Fixing the District of Columbia Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The District transition act remains operative and governs the repeal, transfer, adoption, and amendment chain for the District-related laws it names.

provisions.summary: Short title, purpose, and findings.
provisions.basis: The act may be cited as the Fixing the District of Columbia Act of 2025 and includes extensive findings.
Section 4(a) says the listed laws shall be repealed in their entirety.

provisions.summary: Definitions for the municipal-government transition.
provisions.basis: The section defines municipal government, Metropolitan Police Department, and related transition terms.

provisions.summary: Dissolves the municipal government, transfers MPD to DOJ, transfers DCFEMS to DHS, and reestablishes the D.C. Delegate.
provisions.target: District of Columbia municipal government, MPD, DCFEMS, and Delegate office
provisions.basis: The source says the municipal government is hereby dissolved and the MPD and DCFEMS are hereby transferred.

provisions.summary: Adopts several District municipal laws into federal law with specified amendments.
provisions.target: District Tort Act, DC Criminal Code, Protecting our Firefighters Act, and Fire Marshals Act
provisions.basis: It says those municipal laws are hereby adopted and enacted as federal law with listed amendments.

provisions.summary: Amends Public Law 22-156 to place MPD under DOJ authority and revise leadership language.
provisions.basis: Section 11 says the listed subsections of Public Law 22-156 shall be amended to read.

provisions.summary: Severability and enactment clause.
provisions.basis: The act includes severability and says it takes effect immediately after enactment.

current_implementation.detail: The branch flattens multiple repeal, transfer, and amendment actions into one bloated note instead of preserving the specific actions, and the dump/Trello link remain.

laws.recommended_action: Record the six repeals explicitly, add the listed transfer and amendment notes for the District transition provisions, and remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 37-261 | Federal Criminal Code Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The local source record is usable HTML text, but no verified positive-law code target or approved plan was identified in the reviewed materials.

current_implementation.detail: The enactment text is recoverable in codification/laws/laws/PL-037-261/law.txt and rendered.html, but no verified XML placement is available.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the law as source-reviewed history only until a verified Code target is established.

laws.title: Public Law 38-262 | Fund the Government Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act is temporary appropriations legislation with one repeal of PL 32-230 section 6; it should not become permanent Code text.

provisions.summary: Short title only.
provisions.basis: Fund the Government Act of 2026.

provisions.summary: Definitions for department, appropriations, and emergency fund.
provisions.basis: The section defines the operative terms for the appropriations measure.

provisions.summary: Budget allocations and emergency fund appropriations.
provisions.basis: The text says the funds are hereby appropriated.

provisions.summary: Oversight and accountability for fund misuse.
provisions.basis: The section is oversight and accountability language.

provisions.summary: General Schedule pay scale table.
provisions.basis: The schedule is general and permanent policy language, not a clean positive-law rewrite target.

provisions.summary: Repeals section 6 of PL 32-230 and assigns schedule authority to OPM.
provisions.basis: Section 6 of Public Law 32-230 shall be repealed in its entirety.

provisions.summary: Enactment clause.
provisions.basis: The bill takes effect immediately after passage.

current_implementation.detail: The branch note captures the appropriations act, but it should stay concise and the appropriations text itself should not be turned into permanent Code text.

laws.recommended_action: Exclude the appropriations from permanent Code text, keep only the repeal note for PL 32-230 section 6, and trim the dump/Trello material.

laws.title: Public Law 38-263 | National Security Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: This act is the first half of the CRACK Act replacement: it repeals titles I-IV and leaves titles V-VI alive until PL 38-264 finishes the replacement.

provisions.summary: Short title, findings/purposes, definitions, and severability.
provisions.basis: The opening sections are general statutory-note material.

provisions.summary: Repeals CRACK Act titles I-IV and preserves titles V-VI until this act takes effect.
provisions.basis: Titles I-IV of the CRACK Act of 2022 are repealed.

provisions.summary: National Security Council governance and records preservation.
provisions.basis: The Council provisions are continuing framework language tied to 50 U.S.C. 3021.

provisions.summary: Eligibility determinations, notice, response, review, disclosure limits, and implementation safeguards.
provisions.basis: The eligibility framework is continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Classification limits and in-camera / ex parte procedure safeguards.
provisions.basis: The classification and procedure rules are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Administrative subpoenas and security-sensitive licensing protections.
provisions.basis: The subpoena and licensing provisions are continuing general law.

current_implementation.detail: The branch note captures the CRACK Act national-security replacement, but it remains note-only and still carries dump/Trello cleanup.

laws.recommended_action: Replace the full-text dump with a concise statutory note at the title-50 anchor, remove the Trello link, and if later codification is desired, split the act into actual Title 50 amendments instead of leaving the whole framework in note form.

laws.title: Public Law 38-264 | Organized Crime and Racketeering Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: This act completes the CRACK Act replacement by repealing titles V-VI after PL 38-263 already repealed titles I-IV.

provisions.summary: Short title, findings/purposes, definitions, and severability.
provisions.basis: The opening sections are general statutory-note material.

provisions.summary: Repeals CRACK Act titles V and VI, supplies savings, supersession, and immediate effective date.
provisions.basis: Titles V and VI of the CRACK Act of 2022 are repealed.

provisions.summary: Prohibited racketeering conduct and attempt/conspiracy rule.
provisions.basis: The racketeering prohibitions are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Criminal penalties, forfeiture, and procedural protections.
provisions.basis: The forfeiture and penalty rules are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Civil remedies and no private damages action.
provisions.basis: The civil-remedy limitations are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Public-trust disqualification rules.
provisions.basis: The disqualification rules are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Venue and expedited proceedings.
provisions.basis: The venue and expedited-handling provisions are continuing general law.

current_implementation.detail: The approved plan records the note chain across 18 U.S.C. 1961-1964, and the branch already incorporates the underlying RICO framework.

laws.recommended_action: Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link, keep the repeal history and racketeering framework as a concise statutory note unless and until a precise Title 18 placement is identified, and mark the CRACK Act title repeal in the prior-law history trail.

laws.title: Public Law 38-265 | Employment Clarity and Federal Workforce Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act remains operative as a broad employment framework and repeals earlier employment statutes without self-expiring.

provisions.summary: Short title, findings/purposes, and definitions.
provisions.basis: The opening sections are continuing note material.

provisions.summary: Repeals FERA24, the Intelligence Community Employment Act, and the redundant separation framework.
provisions.basis: The named prior laws are repealed to the extent stated.

provisions.summary: Severability and immediate effective date.
provisions.basis: The act takes effect immediately and contains severability.

provisions.summary: Private-sector at-will default, discrimination/retaliation limits, and notice rules.
provisions.basis: The at-will default rule is continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Federal service categories, adverse-action process, provisional service, at-will agreements, emergency suspension, internal review, and judicial review.
provisions.basis: The federal employment rules are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Intelligence-community at-will exceptions and security-clearance access limits.
provisions.basis: The security-clearance and access language is continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Dual-employment and conflict-of-interest restrictions with enforcement.
provisions.basis: The conflict-of-interest rules are continuing general law.

provisions.summary: Bureau enforcement, private remedies, anti-retaliation, and limitation periods.
provisions.basis: The enforcement and remedy provisions are continuing general law.

current_implementation.detail: The branch keeps the employment framework as a Title 5 note; no exact Code target is identified and the dump/Trello link remain.

laws.recommended_action: Remove the full-text dump and Trello link, retain a concise statutory note at a Title 5 employment anchor, and if the repository later codifies this framework, move the direct amendments into actual Title 5 and related-title text.
